story.text: As a session working in a git worktree, I need every .claude hook script path in settings.json rooted at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, because the cwd-relative form 'python .claude/hooks/X.py' makes a session whose cwd is a worktree lacking that (newer) script exit python with code 2 — which Claude Code reads as a PreToolUse BLOCK, hard-blocking every Edit/Write/Bash in that worktree (issue #235, the 100%-worktree-break). I want safe worktree isolation, the prerequisite for paying down the coordination tax.

briefing.headline: hook-path-rooting — root every .claude hook path at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR so worktree sessions stop hard-blocking (fix #235)
briefing.value: The hooks in .claude/settings.json were wired as 'python .claude/hooks/X.py' — a cwd-relative path. A session whose cwd is a worktree missing that script makes python exit 2 (file-not-found), which Claude Code treats as a PreToolUse deny, so every Edit/Write/Bash in the worktree is hard-blocked. Rooting each script path at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (the primary tree, always present, cwd-independent — the documented CC idiom) removes the break. The 'python -m loop.*' module calls are left as-is: loop/ exists in every worktree, so they never hard-block.
briefing.why_now: Issue #235 is open and live; the break hit three times in one session and blocks ALL worktree isolation — the exact mechanism the session-gateway arc needs to pay down the coordination tax. This is the bootstrap: landing it unblocks every future worktree session immediately, rather than waiting for the larger terminal-pull-gateway stack (PR #226) that carries the full fix on top.
briefing.if_accepted: Worktree sessions stop hard-blocking on missing newer hook scripts; safe isolation becomes usable; the coordination tax starts being paid down.
briefing.if_rejected: Worktree isolation stays a session-breaker; sessions keep colliding on the shared viewport tree (the poison this very fix had to route around to land).
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: a one-file settings.json change rooting paths at a documented, always-present variable; reverting is one revert. No code logic changes.
briefing.mechanism: Rewrite each hook command in .claude/settings.json from 'python .claude/<path>.py <args>' to 'python "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/<path>.py" <args>'. Applied to main's current hook set (17 commands); the not-yet-on-main hooks (change_guard, ask_guard) arrive already rooted with their introducing stack, so this is forward-compatible. Mirrors the intent of the stranded commit 9bd1cfd, which could not cherry-pick cleanly because main's settings.json lacks those hooks.

_note: Birth record for hook-path-rooting — fix #235, root every .claude hook path at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (done-line 0128). Never mutated; verdicts are the fold over .ai-native/log/ (D-5).
